title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
The spread (...) and rest (...) operators share the same syntax but serve opposite purposes.

Spread — expands an iterable (array, object, string) into individual elements:
• Copy arrays/objects
• Merge arrays/objects
• Pass array elements as function arguments

Rest — collects remaining elements into an array:
• In function parameters: gather extra arguments
• In destructuring: gather remaining items

Note: both are shallow — nested objects/arrays are still references.
